DF Wu
GitHub LinkedIn Website

Backend & Platform Engineer · Taipei, Taiwan

Systems designed for clarity, built for the team that comes after me.

Building backend systems that stay stable, maintainable, and deployable as complexity grows.

About

I focus on what happens after you ship. Will the person who inherits this codebase understand what I was thinking? Will the ops team trust it at 3am?

I write code for the maintenance window, not the demo. Architecture should make teams faster, not trap them. Complexity is a cost — pay it only when you must.

I'm active in Taiwan's civic tech community through g0v.social, and I run a home-lab where the infrastructure is my own code.

How I Join Teams

Month 1 Map architecture boundaries. Find where the team loses time.
Month 2 Small, targeted fixes. Reduce friction before adding features.
Month 3 Measurable reliability gains. Nobody stayed late because of my code.

Interested in working together?

I'm best suited for teams that value maintainability, stability, and calm execution.
